investigations.title: Snowball Effect
investigations.hypothesis: Game-1 winners take the series at a rate significantly above 50%
investigations.method: Conditional probability P(series win | game 1 win), Fisher's exact test (one-sided)
investigations.finding: Game-1 winners took the series 89.0% of the time (121/136)

investigations.title: Positional Advantage
investigations.hypothesis: The first-listed player (Player 1) wins at a rate significantly different from 50%
investigations.method: Binomial test of Player-1 win rate vs 50%, with per-map breakdown

investigations.title: Fatigue Factor
investigations.hypothesis: Higher-ELO player win rate declines in later games of a series
investigations.method: Stratified win rates by game position (1 / 2 / 3+), chi-square test
investigations.test_name: Chi-square test on 3x2 contingency (higher-ELO win/loss by bucket)

investigations.title: Comfort Picks vs. Wild Cards
investigations.hypothesis: Comfort picks (top-3 most-played civs) outperform one-off picks
investigations.method: Per-player paired win rates, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (one-sided)

investigations.title: Clutch Factor
investigations.hypothesis: Some players significantly over/underperform in deciding games
investigations.method: Per-player binomial test comparing clutch win rate to overall baseline
investigations.finding: 1 player(s) with statistically significant clutch deviation (p < 0.05)
investigations.test_name: Per-player binomial vs overall WR (two-sided); multiple comparisons
investigations.multiple_testing_note: 20 implicit tests; Bonferroni alpha ~0.0025 would be stricter than 0.05 per test

investigations.title: One-Sided Civ Matchups
investigations.hypothesis: Some civ pairings have significantly imbalanced win rates
investigations.method: Civ-vs-civ win rate matrix (min 3 games), binomial test per pair
investigations.finding: 0 matchup(s) significantly one-sided out of 25 tested
investigations.test_name: Binomial per pair (one-sided vs 50%); multiple comparisons across pairs

investigations.title: Map Specialists
investigations.hypothesis: Certain players have statistically significant map affinities
investigations.method: Per-player per-map win rate vs. baseline, binomial test (min 3 games)
investigations.finding: 1 significant map affinity pair(s) out of 196
investigations.test_name: Per cell binomial vs player baseline (two-sided); multiple comparisons
investigations.multiple_testing_note: ~196 cells; expect ~9 false positives at alpha 0.05 under global null

investigations.title: Upset Probability
investigations.hypothesis: Tournament upsets exceed the logistic ELO model expectation
investigations.method: Bin by ELO difference, compare actual upset rate to E(win) = 1/(1+10^(-d/400)), binomial test
investigations.test_name: Binomial test (upset count vs mean expected upset rate)

investigations.title: Tempo Control
investigations.hypothesis: Players with low duration variance (consistent tempo) win more
investigations.method: Tercile-based tempo grouping on mean duration, Spearman correlation (CV vs win rate)
investigations.test_name: Spearman correlation (duration CV vs win rate across players)

investigations.title: Meta Evolution
investigations.hypothesis: Civ pick distributions change across tournament stages
investigations.method: Per-civ pick rate by stage, chi-square test
investigations.finding: Only one stage in data -- cannot measure evolution
